definitions.definition: means the process which establishes, recognises and confirms with certainty and with respect to any particular parcel or plot of land , what rights and interests exist, by whom they are exercised and to what limitations, if any, they are subject

for purposes of expropriation of land for a public purpose or servitudes means a Government Ministry or department or an agent named in the notice of declaration to hold the land or benefit from the servitude for a public purpose

means land used exclusively or mainly for agriculture, whether as arable, pasture, grazing, orchard or seed growing, or for fish farming, forestry (including afforestation), or for the breeding or keeping of livestock, including any creature kept for the production of food, wool, silk, skins or fur
means such Local Council or other agency empowered to make allocations under this Act
means the granting of a right to occupy and use land
means a person other than the holder of a lease to whom an allocation of land is made under this Act

includes arbitration, conciliation, mediation, settlement out of court and negotiation whether done in accordance with the rules and principles of the customary law or in accordance with the rules and principles of the common law or in accordance with statutory procedures or by private agreement between the parties
means a general land use plan approved in accordance with the Town and Country Planning Act 1980 1 ; 1 Act No. 11 of 1980 "
includes the Chamber of Commerce or any other business affiliation
carries the same meaning as in the Chieftainship Act 1968 2 ; 2 Act No. of 1968 "
means the Commissioner for Lands

means the certification or authorisation by the Commissioner to the applicant to deal in or to engage in a transaction on land under section 35 which is meant for the preservation of the integrity of the land tenure system in Lesotho
means the offering, giving, receiving, or soliciting of, directly or indirectly, anything of value to influence improperly the actions of another party in rendering of land services
has the meaning ascribed to it by the Deeds Registry Act 1967
means the delineation by means of a land survey of boundaries of a plot of land
means a provisional cadastral map prepared in respect of an adjudication section pending finality
means the District Land Court established by section 75
means any act or omission, including misrepresentation, that knowingly or recklessly misleads, or attempts to mislead, a party to obtain financial or other benefit or to avoid an obligation
means— (a) partnership in which any partner is a non-citizen; or (b) a body corporate in which any of the directors or shareholders is a non-citizen
includes land covered with water, all things natural or man-made growing on land, and buildings or other structures permanently affixed or attached to land
means the Land Court established under section 74
means a right granted or issued under this Act and the instrument evidencing the same
carries the same meaning as Local Authority in the Local Government Act 1997 3 ; 3 Act No. 12 of 1967 "
means the Minister responsible for land
when used as a noun, and unless the context otherwise requires, means a written notice
means all the encumbrances, in interests, rights and powers capable of overriding first registration or subsequent registration or an allocation , without itself being registered
includes any company or association or body of persons, corporate or incorporate
includes water and sewerage pipelines
means a servitude created by agreement between private parties with the consent of the Commissioner
means prescribed by this Act or regulations made pursuant to this Act
means any public body or officer or any official or any other person empowered under any law to discharge land duties, whether as a short term or long term assignment
means the circumstances listed in section 51
has the meaning ascribed to it in section 50
means a servitude granted by the Minister under section 53

means title to land which has been allocated: (a) for commercial or industrial purposes; (b) for purpose of an ecclesiastical, benevolent, charitable or educational institution of a public character; (c) for purpose of a hospital, clinic or dispensary; (d) for such other purposes as the Minister may, by notice published in the Gazette , declare; (e) for residential and agricultural purposes in urban areas; or (f) a title deed registered under the Deeds Registry Act 1967
means the Registrar of Deeds
and all derivatives of that word means registration in the Deeds Registry
means regulations made under this Act

means— (a) the process of surveying, planning, adjudicating and registering the boundaries and rights associated with a parcel of land informally occupied or; (b) readjustment of boundaries for the purpose of town planning
means an area which is not an urban area
has the meaning ascribed to it under section 9
means a right attached to a parcel of land which is the subject of a lease either to use other land in a particular manner or to restrict its use to a particular extent
means the adjudication of a specific parcel of land undertaken in isolation from any contiguous or surrounding parcels of land
means land , the boundaries, area and situation of which have been surveyed to the standard required for a lease of such land to be registered

means the ascertainment of rights and interests of a defined area of land that consists of more than one parcel of land in which the boundaries and extent of each parcel of land within the area is demarcated and surveyed and the rights and interests therein recorded
means land which has not been surveyed
